title: Vitals reading
description: Flat fields matching a typical VitalsRecord shape. Every field independent and optional — extract whatever the speaker mentioned.

fields.label: Temperature (°F)
fields.helpText: Body temperature in Fahrenheit. Range 90–110. Extract from any phrasing — "temperature 99", "temperature is 99", "temp of 99", "T 99" all → 99. If speaker said Celsius, convert to °F (C × 9/5 + 32). If two different temperatures stated, take the FIRST one. Omit if not stated.

fields.label: Pulse (beats/min)
fields.helpText: Heart rate / pulse, integer 20–300. Any phrasing — "pulse 104", "pulse is 104", "HR 88", "heart rate of 92", "HR was 70". Omit if not stated.

fields.label: Respiratory rate (/min)
fields.helpText: Respiratory / breathing rate, integer 4–80. Any phrasing — "RR 22", "RR is 22", "respiratory rate 16", "breathing 18", "breaths 20 per minute". Omit if not stated.

fields.label: BP — systolic (mmHg)
fields.helpText: Systolic BP, integer 40–300. From "BP 130/85" or "BP 130 over 85" → 130. PARTIAL BP REQUIRED — "BP 120" alone (no diastolic) → 120; "BP is 150" → 150; "pressure 140" → 140. ALWAYS extract systolic when ANY blood pressure is stated, even without a diastolic. Omit only if no BP was stated.

fields.label: BP — diastolic (mmHg)
fields.helpText: Diastolic BP, integer 20–200. From "BP 130/85" → 85. ONLY emit when the speaker stated both numbers (slash / "over" / "by"). Omit if only systolic was spoken — partial BP is normal.

fields.label: SpO₂ (%)
fields.helpText: Oxygen saturation, integer 50–100. Any phrasing — "SpO2 96", "SpO2 is 96", "saturation 96", "saturation is 88", "sats 99", "oxygen 98 percent". Lower values (e.g. 88) are clinically concerning but VALID — extract them. Omit if not stated.

fields.label: Pain score (0–10)
fields.helpText: Numerical Rating Scale 0–10, integer. Explicit values — "pain 4", "pain score 8", "pain score is 8", "NRS 6". Explicit zero — "no pain" / "pain free" → 0. CRITICAL: if the speaker did NOT say "pain" / "NRS" / "score" at all, OMIT the field — never default to 0.
